meta.tagline: Visual, verified walkthroughs of ownCloud Infinite Scale Web — every step is a real end-to-end test, captured, not mocked.

tutorials.title: Searching your files
tutorials.summary: Find files by name or by what's inside them, then narrow the results with filters.

steps.title: Start from the search bar
steps.caption: Wherever you are in ownCloud Web, the search bar sits at the very top. Click it and begin typing what you're looking for.

steps.title: Results appear as you type
steps.caption: Matches surface instantly in a dropdown. Pick one to jump straight to the file, or press <kbd>Enter</kbd> to open the full results page.

steps.title: It searches inside your files too
steps.caption: Results match on the file name and the file's contents. Here annual-summary.txt matches because the word report appears inside it — shown highlighted.

steps.title: Narrow by when it changed
steps.caption: Open the Last modified filter to limit results to today, the last 7 or 30 days, this year, or last year.

steps.title: Match the file name only
steps.caption: Switch from Full text search to Title only to match file names alone. Content-only matches like annual-summary.txt drop away — leaving just budget-report.txt.

tutorials.title: Sharing a file with a colleague
tutorials.summary: Give someone in your organisation access to a file, choose what they can do, and keep track of it.

steps.title: Open the share panel
steps.caption: Hover a file and click the Share icon. The sharing panel opens on the right, ready to add people.

steps.title: Find your colleague
steps.caption: Type a name or email in the Search box. Matching people in your organisation appear — pick the right one.

steps.title: Choose what they can do
steps.caption: Set the permission: <mark>Can view</mark> lets them view and download; <mark>Can edit</mark> also lets them upload and change the file.

steps.title: Send the invite
steps.caption: Click Share. Your colleague now appears under Shared with with exactly the access you chose.

steps.title: Keep track of what you've shared
steps.caption: Everything you share lives under Shares → Shared with others, where you can adjust the role or revoke access at any time.

tutorials.title: Creating a project Space
tutorials.summary: Set up a shared Space for a team or project, with its own storage and members — separate from your personal files.

steps.title: Start a new Space
steps.caption: Go to Spaces and click New Space. Give it a clear name — here, Project Apollo — then create it.

steps.title: Your Space is ready
steps.caption: The new Space appears in your Spaces overview with its own storage quota — independent of your personal files.

steps.title: Open the members panel
steps.caption: Click Show members on the Space to manage who belongs to it. You start out as the Manager.

steps.title: Invite your team
steps.caption: Search for a colleague, pick a role, and click Add. Here Marie joins Project Apollo with Can view access.

tutorials.title: Organising files with tags
tutorials.summary: Label files with keywords so you can group and find them later — across folders and Spaces.

steps.title: Open a file's details
steps.caption: Select a file and open the details panel on the right. Among the file's details you'll find a Tags field.

steps.title: Add a tag
steps.caption: Type a label — like <mark>quarterly</mark> — and press <kbd>Enter</kbd>. Reuse an existing tag or create a new one on the fly.

steps.title: Tagged and findable
steps.caption: The tag sticks to the file. Later you can pull up everything with that label by searching tag:quarterly.

tutorials.title: Restoring a deleted file
tutorials.summary: Deleted something by mistake? Files aren't gone immediately — recover them from Deleted files.

steps.title: Find your deleted files
steps.caption: Open Deleted files from the left sidebar. Removed items wait here instead of disappearing for good.

steps.title: Select and restore
steps.caption: Tick the file you want back and click <mark>Restore</mark>.

steps.title: It's back
steps.caption: The file returns to its original location in your Personal space — exactly where it was before.
